summary: Matched a batch of duplicated Kirby copy-ability routines in ftKb_Init, mainly SpecialN hat cleanup/load helpers and repeated collision-state transitions for copied specials. The PR body frames this as the remaining low-hanging duplicate work in ftKb; the decomp.dev bot reported 54 new matches and +7088 matched bytes for GALE01.

key_files.reason: Main matching work: replaced many nonmatching placeholders with matched duplicate implementations for Kirby SpecialN hat setup/teardown and copied-special collision callbacks.
Removed the nonmatching marker for ftCo_8009D704, indicating the existing implementation is now considered matched/reusable.
Moved ftCo_8009D704 from static/private declaration to public prototype so ftKb_Init.c can call it.

what_changed: Added include of "ef/eflib.h" to ftKb_Init.c so DK-copy collision helper code can assign efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll callbacks.
Introduced static helper ftKb_SpecialN_800EFAF0_inline to centralize Kirby hat cleanup: remove the hat JObj, clear fv.kb.hat.jobj, free fighter_x2040_alloc_data, and clear fv.kb.hat.x14.data.

Matched multiple trivial hat-teardown wrappers by calling ftKb_SpecialN_800EFAF0_inline, including ftKb_SpecialN_800EFBFC, ftKb_SpecialN_800EFD08, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0168, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0274, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0380, ftKb_SpecialN_800F05A8, ftKb_SpecialN_800F06B4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F07C0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0C20, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F0E48.

Matched hat teardown plus dynamic-bone unload wrappers such as ftKb_SpecialN_800EFE1C, ftKb_SpecialN_800EFF38, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0054, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0494, ftKb_SpecialN_800F08D4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F09F0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0B0C, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0D34, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F0F5C.

Matched repeated Kirby hat-load routines for several copied fighter kinds by indexing ft_80459B88.hats and using KirbyHatStruct fields hat_joint and joint_num. Examples in the diff include FTKIND_KOOPA, FTKIND_LINK, FTKIND_NANA, FTKIND_LUIGI, FTKIND_MARS, FTKIND_ZELDA, FTKIND_FALCO, and FTKIND_GANON.

Matched several Samus-copy collision functions such as ftKb_SsSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_SsSpecialNCancel_Coll, ftKb_SsSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_SsSpecialAirNStart_Coll, and ftKb_SsSpecialAirN_Coll using ft_80082708 ground/air checks, ftCommon_8007D5D4, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, and ftKirbyDmgInline.
Added static ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Coll_inline to restore Kirby/DK-copy callbacks after state transitions: death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, pre_hitlag_cb, and post_hitlag_cb.

Matched repeated DK-copy collision functions such as ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialNLoop_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialNCancel_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialNFull_Coll, ftKb_DkSpecialAirNStart_Coll, and ftKb_DkSpecialAirNLoop_Coll.
Matched additional repeated copied-special collision routines for Sk and Mt groups, including Start, Loop, LoopFull, Cancel, End, and Air variants visible in the diff excerpt.
Exposed ftCo_8009D704 through ftdynamics.h and removed its nonmatching marker in ftdynamics.c so ftKb_Init.c can call it directly.
smart_moves: Used local static helpers with an _inline suffix to express duplicated compiler-generated patterns without forcing a broad refactor of unrelated call sites.
Factored the hat-freeing sequence once and made many wrapper functions match by preserving their tiny call-only shape.

For hat load routines, replaced placeholder-ish access through ft_80459B88.x0->x0 and HSD_Joint with typed KirbyHatStruct access through ft_80459B88.hats[FTKIND_*], hat->hat_joint, and hat->joint_num.
Changed the ftParts_8007487C arguments from temporary untyped hat_x14/PAD_STACK style to direct field addresses, notably &fp->fv.kb.hat.x14.count, matching the repeated hat setup pattern.
Reused ftCo_8009D704 rather than duplicating its behavior in ftKb_Init.c, and adjusted ftdynamics.h visibility accordingly.
Kept repeated collision functions explicit instead of overabstracting them; this preserves per-function motion IDs and flag constants that matter for matching.
decomp_lessons: When many functions are address-named duplicates, first identify the highest-count repeated bodies; the PR author explicitly described this as harvesting low-hanging duplicate fruit.
Small static helpers can be a matching tool: they may allow several thin wrappers to match while keeping the emitted code shape close to the original.
If a function from another translation unit is needed for a match, promote its prototype carefully in the header rather than reimplementing or using placeholder declarations.
Typed struct field recovery can remove PAD_STACK/UNK_T scaffolding and make duplicate families easier to recognize.

Kirby copied-special collision callbacks often follow a compact pattern: test ft_80082708 against GA_Ground or GA_Air, call ftCommon_8007D5D4, change motion with current animation frame, then restore Kirby-specific damage/callback state.

Callback restoration is part of matching some copied-special collision transitions; DK-copy routines in this PR reset death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, pre_hitlag_cb, and post_hitlag_cb after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
For future ftKb work, remaining duplicates may be less profitable according to the PR body: pairs/triples or simple patterns that appear broadly, like ftKb_PeSpecialLw_Phys.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown or partially named functions remain in the style ftKb_SpecialN_800EFAF0 and ftCo_8009D704.
Static matching helpers introduced here use the pattern existing_function_or_family_name + _inline, e.g. ftKb_SpecialN_800EFAF0_inline and ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Coll_inline.
Copied-special Kirby routines use ftKb_<copy-prefix>Special... names, with prefixes visible in this PR including Ss, Dk, Sk, and Mt.
Collision callbacks use the _Coll suffix, physics callbacks use _Phys, and air variants include Air in the function name.
Fighter-kind array indexing uses FTKIND_* constants such as FTKIND_LINK, FTKIND_LUIGI, FTKIND_ZELDA, and FTKIND_GANON.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted repeated placeholder markers into C bodies by copying a known duplicate pattern and varying only the fighter kind, motion state ID, or ground/air condition.
Matched tiny wrapper functions by extracting common code into static helper calls rather than repeating full bodies everywhere.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in condition/setup patterns, but also preserved gobj->user_data assignments where the emitted code shape required it.
Used exact numeric motion IDs and flags in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, such as 0x19B, 0x19C, 0x1CC through 0x1D0, and flags like 0x0C4C5080U or 0x0C4D508EU.

For hat-load duplicates, used ft_80459B88.hats[FTKIND_*] plus HSD_ObjAlloc, ftParts_80074148, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, ftParts_80074170, ftParts_80075650, ftParts_8007487C, and ftCo_8009D704 in the same repeated sequence.
Added a public prototype for ftCo_8009D704 to support cross-file reuse and remove a local nonmatch marker.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increase to 45.61%, +0.18%, +7088 bytes, with 54 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining ftKb_Init placeholder functions with duplicate collision bodies limited to pairs or triples.
Search for other files that can reuse ftCo_8009D704 now that it has a public prototype.
Audit Kirby hat-load routines for more opportunities to replace UNK_T/PAD_STACK scaffolding with KirbyHatStruct fields.
Compare ftKb_PeSpecialLw_Phys-style simple duplicate patterns across copied-special implementations before deciding whether abstraction helps matching.
